dict.string:
# Workflow Finder Alfred Workflow
Finds all your installed workflows and perform actions on them.
![](images/showcase_1.png)
If you want a workflow to find workflows from the [Alfred Gallery](https://alfred.app/), you should use [Alfred Gallery](https://alfred.app/workflows/alfredapp/alfred-gallery/) instead.
## Usage - Search for workflows via the keyword `wflow` (configurable from [configuration](https://www.alfredapp.com/help/workflows/user-configuration/) pane).
- You can also search for workflows whose description contains the keyword or search for workflows by author.
![](images/showcase_2.png)
- <kbd>↩︎</kbd> to edit the selected workflow. - <kbd>⌘</kbd><kbd>↩︎</kbd> to open the configuration window for the workflow. - <kbd>⌘</kbd><kbd>⇧</kbd><kbd>↩︎</kbd> to copy the bundle ID for the workflow. - <kbd>⇧</kbd><kbd>↩︎</kbd> to pass the workflow folder for the workflow to [File Action](https://www.alfredapp.com/blog/tips-and-tricks/file-actions-from-alfred-or-finder/). - <kbd>⌃</kbd><kbd>↩︎</kbd> to pass the data folder for the workflow to File Action. - <kbd>⌥</kbd><kbd>⌃</kbd><kbd>↩︎</kbd> to pass the cache folder for the workflow to File Action. - <kbd>⇧</kbd><kbd>⌃</kbd><kbd>↩︎</kbd> to trash the workflow (can be undone in the Trash). - <kbd>⌥</kbd><kbd>⇧</kbd><kbd>⌘</kbd><kbd>↩︎</kbd> to copy the title, bundle ID, version etc. for the workflow.
- <kbd>⌥</kbd> to show all actions above for the selected workflow — including export the workflow to an `.alfredworkflow` file.
![](images/showcase_3.png)
The copied info will contain title, bundle ID, version of the workflow (not this workflow), Alfred version, macOS version. Good for asking help about a workflow on the [Alfred Forum](https://www.alfredforum.com/forum/1-alfred-workflows/).
Info for this workflow:
`Workflow Finder・v0.1, bundle ID: com.may-june-july.listworkflows . Alfred version: 5.5.1 2273. macOS: 15.3.1.`
Choose whether to open the workflow (data, cache) folder in Finder or to pass it to Alfred
Categories to exclude from results. One line per category.
